items.summary: While no one expected the Fed to cut — much less hike — at this meeting, the market always looks for clues about what's next.

items.headline: Fed votes to hold rates steady, notes 'uncertain' impacts from Iran war
items.summary: The Federal Reserve on Wednesday released its decision in interest rates.

items.headline: Bank of Japan keeps rates steady as expected, warns Iran war may push up inflation
items.summary: The Bank of Japan kept its rates steady at 0.75% as expected, but noted that inflation risks now are tilted to the upside due to the Iran war.

items.headline: CNBC Daily Open: Markets reel as Fed holds rates steady and Iran hits a key Qatar gas hub
items.summary: Global markets slid after the Federal Reserve held rates steady and warned of persistent inflation, while an Iranian missile strike caused “extensive damage” to Qatar’s Ras Laffan LNG hub, heightening fears of a fresh energy shock.

items.headline: Asia markets track Wall Street losses as Iran war fuels energy worries; BOJ rate decision on deck
items.summary: Investors in Asia will look toward the Bank of Japan decision, which is expected to hold rates at 0.75%.

items.headline: Oil jumps 4% as Iranian retaliatory strikes on Qatar’s key energy facility stoke supply worries
items.summary: Oil prices jumped as markets react to escalating tensions after Qatar said Iranian missile strikes damaged a key liquefied natural gas export facility.

items.headline: How China is getting everyone on OpenClaw, from gearheads to grandmas

items.headline: Iran missile attack on Qatar causes 'extensive damage' to facility housing huge gas plant
items.summary: Iran's Revolutionary Guard had threatened to attack energy facilities in Qatar, Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

items.headline: A Labubu movie is on its way as Pop Mart expands the iconic toy franchise

items.headline: Social media is eroding young people’s happiness. Low-income teens may be most at risk.
items.summary: Heavy usage of certain types of social media may be contributing to a drop in youth well-being, according to Gallup’s new World Happiness Report.

items.headline: Why Starbucks’ turnaround plans might not win over the younger crowd

items.headline: Five Below stock continues its tear, as upbeat outlook eases fears of consumer slowdown

items.headline: Lumentum’s new revenue forecast further fuels one of the market’s hottest stocks

items.headline: Powell offers his clearest statement yet on staying at the Fed even as leadership shifts
